message:
fix(combinator): poll both Select branches for cancel-correctness

Fix a cancel-safety violation in Select<A, B> where the losing branch
was never polled, preventing JoinFuture creation and causing task leaks
when Select was dropped:

Previously, Select used short-circuit polling — returning immediately
when the left branch was Ready without ever polling the right branch.
This meant if the right branch wraps a JoinFuture, that future was never
created, so its Drop couldn't abort the spawned task.

Now Select polls both futures on each iteration (matching SelectAll
behavior), storing both results before checking readiness. The left
branch still wins ties. This ensures losers are at least polled once
to initialize their drop guards, satisfying the "losers are drained"
invariant.

Updated test assertion from 0 polls to 1 poll for the loser branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
